seo.pageTitle: Suwon Hwaseong, Gwangmyeong Cave & Starfield Tour | AtoC Korea
seo.metaDescription: 9-hour small-group day tour from Seoul: UNESCO Suwon Hwaseong Fortress, Hwaseong Haenggung, Suwon Nammun Market, Starfield Library, LED-lit Gwangmyeong Cave.
seo.title: 수원화성·광명동굴·스타필드 소그룹 투어 | AtoC Korea
seo.description: 서울 출발 경기도 소그룹 투어(월요일 제외): 수원화성(유네스코) → 화성행궁 → 스타필드 별마당도서관 → 광명동굴(폐금광 LED 테마파크). 전 입장료 포함.

catalog_card.title: 수원화성·광명동굴·스타필드 소그룹 투어
catalog_card.shortCardDescription: 수원화성(유네스코 세계문화유산), 화성행궁, 스타필드 수원 별마당도서관, 광명동굴(1912년 금광 폐갱 개조 LED 테마파크)을 소그룹으로 하루에 돌아봅니다. 월요일 미운행. 전 입장료 포함.
catalog_card.subtitle: 서울에서 경기도 경유 소그룹 문화 당일 투어 — 유네스코 성곽, 조선 이궁, 별마당도서관, 지하 LED 테마파크의 네 가지 대조적인 면을 하루에.

headlineLine2: 경기도에서 가장 많이 사진 찍히는 하루, 9시간에

price.priceNote: Original price 60 USD on GetYourGuide; AtoC Korea sale price is a flat 10% discount.

hero.tagline: 한국 유일의 완전한 성곽 도시, 예술로 재탄생한 옛 금광, 전국에서 가장 많이 촬영되는 도서관

galleryItems.alt: Suwon Hwaseong Fortress wall section with Janganmun gate visible in the distance and bastions along the ridgeline
galleryItems.caption: Hwaseong Fortress — UNESCO since 1997
galleryItems.location: Suwon Hwaseong Fortress, Janganmun-Hwaseomun section, Suwon
galleryItems.atmosphere: Stone walls built 1794-1796 under King Jeongjo; only walled fortress city remaining in Korea

galleryItems.alt: Hwaseong Haenggung Palace courtyard during a royal-guard reenactment with Joseon-era armor and traditional drums
galleryItems.caption: Haenggung — royal guard reenactment
galleryItems.location: Hwaseong Haenggung Palace, inside the fortress, Suwon
galleryItems.atmosphere: Korea's largest haenggung; daily royal-guard ceremonies and martial-arts demos

galleryItems.alt: Gwangmyeong Cave's LED-lit Golden Road tunnel with curving illuminated walls inside the former colonial-era gold mine
galleryItems.caption: Gwangmyeong Cave — Golden Road tunnel
galleryItems.atmosphere: Former Siheung gold mine (1912-1972); 7.8 km of tunnels; constant 12°C year-round

galleryItems.alt: The 41-meter dragon sculpture inside Gwangmyeong Cave's main chamber with its head curved upward toward LED lighting
galleryItems.caption: 41-m dragon sculpture — Korea's largest
galleryItems.atmosphere: Korea's largest dragon (41 m, 800 kg, 'Lord of the Cave') built by Weta Workshop (Lord of the Rings prop studio)

galleryItems.alt: Starfield Library Suwon's four-story atrium with 22-meter floor-to-ceiling bookshelves and reading lounges arranged on the lower floors
galleryItems.caption: Starfield Library Suwon — 22 m bookshelves
galleryItems.atmosphere: Shinsegae's flagship 2nd-generation Starfield, opened January 2024; free entry; vinyl-record listening area

galleryItems.alt: Suwon Nammun Market's covered alley with food stalls selling Suwon-style galbi, fried chicken, and traditional sweets
galleryItems.caption: Suwon Nammun Market — 220-year King Jeongjo market
galleryItems.location: Suwon Nammun Market, south gate of the fortress, Suwon
galleryItems.atmosphere: 220-year history; created by King Jeongjo during Hwaseong's construction; tongdak alley + 9 sub-markets

itineraryStops.name: 서울 출발
itineraryStops.description: 서울 중심 2개 집결 지점: 홍익대학교역 8번 출구(홍대) 또는 명동역 2·4번 출구. 경부고속도로로 수원 이동.
highlights: Pickup from Hongik University (Line 2) or Myeongdong (Line 4) ~08:30
1-hour coach south to Suwon via Gyeongbu Expressway
9-hour day designed to clear Hwaseong before crowds peak
Coach with air conditioning + USB charging
timeUsed: Meet at designated Seoul pickup point (~5 min for boarding)
1-hour coach drive to Suwon (~60 min in light traffic)
itineraryStops.whyOnRoute: An 09:30 Suwon arrival puts the group at Hwaseong Fortress with 60-90 min before midday crowds peak around 11:00. The day's clockwork — fortress → palace → market lunch → library → cave — minimizes coach repositioning and ends with the cave's reliable indoor finale.

smartNotes.tip: Confirm exact pickup time and meeting point at booking. Bring water, sunscreen, and comfortable shoes for fortress wall walking — and a light jacket for the cave (12°C year-round).

itineraryStops.name: 수원화성 (유네스코 세계문화유산)
itineraryStops.description: 수원화성은 1997년 유네스코 세계문화유산으로 등재된 한국 유일의 완전한 형태의 조선시대 성곽 도시입니다. 정조 대왕이 1794~1796년에 축성했으며, 공사 전 과정을 담은 10권 분량의 『화성성역의궤』(별도 유네스코 기록유산 등재)가 현존합니다. 전체 성벽 5.7km 중 약 1km 구간(장안문~화서문, 공심돈·봉돈 포함)을 걷습니다. 4대문(장안문·팔달문·화서문·창룡문), 포루·각루·수문 48개 시설물이 보존되어 있습니다. 입장료 포함. 방문 시간: 약 60분.
highlights: **UNESCO World Heritage Site since 1997** — Korea's only walled fortress city
Built 1794-1796 in 2 years 9 months under King Jeongjo
Designed by Jeong Yak-yong using Korean + Chinese + Western fortification science
Hwaseong Seongyeok Uigwe — UNESCO Memory of the World 2007
5.74 km circumference, 41 strategic military structures
Janganmun north gate — **largest existing royal gate in Korea**
1.2 km guided walk Janganmun → Hwaseomun (highest photo-density section)
Hwahongmun 7-arch sluice gate — most photographed structure
FREE for hanbok-wearers + seniors 65+ + last Wed of month
timeUsed: Photo time + reassembly at vehicle (~10 min)
Optional Hwaseong Yeolcha sightseeing trolley Yeonmudae↔Paldalmun (~30 min one-way; ₩4,000 adult)
itineraryStops.whyOnRoute: The route's UNESCO anchor — Korea's only remaining walled fortress city, built 1794-1796 under King Jeongjo. The Janganmun → Hwaseomun walk gives the highest density of preserved military structures in a 60-min stop, sized for a 9-hour day with 5 substantive stops. The full 5.74 km loop would consume 2.5+ hours and cannibalize the day's other heritage and modern stops. For this 5-stop itinerary, Hwaseong serves as the **morning anchor** — the 5.74 km perimeter is too long to circumnavigate in a single visit, so the day-trip format focuses on the Janganmun → Seojangdae western section (~25-30 min walking, highest preservation density). The 1801 **Hwaseong Seongyeok Uigwe** construction record — UNESCO Memory of the World since 2007 — documented every stone, beam, and laborer's wage, enabling faithful 1975-onwards reconstruction after Korean War damage. Jeong Yak-yong's **Geojunggi (거중기) pulley-and-block crane** is the world's first documented use of Western mechanical-advantage engineering on a Joseon-era construction site, predating Korea's industrial mechanization by ~120 years.

visitBasics.hours: Wall path 24 hours; recommended 09:00-18:00 summer / 09:00-17:00 winter
visitBasics.admission: Adults 1,000 / Youth 700 / Children 500 KRW (FREE: children ≤6, seniors 65+, hanbok-wearers, last Wed of month)
visitBasics.walking: Moderate — wall has steep sections; stairs at gates

smartNotes.photo: **Hwahongmun (north sluice gate)** with 7 arches over Suwoncheon Stream — the iconic shot. **Janganmun (largest existing royal gate in Korea)** at golden hour. **Seojangdae (west command post)** sunset over Paldalsan Mountain.
smartNotes.facilities: Visitor Information Centers at all main gates; English-Korean signage throughout
smartNotes.tip: Hanbok rental at Haenggung Square gives FREE fortress + palace admission. The Janganmun → Hwaseomun section gives the highest per-meter heritage density.

sources: UNESCO World Heritage Centre — Suwon Hwaseong (Criteria ii/iii inscription, 1997)
Korea Heritage Service / Cultural Heritage Administration (cha.go.kr — Hwaseong Fortress + Hwaseong Seongyeok Uigwe Memory of the World 2007)
Korea Tourism Organization (english.visitkorea.or.kr — Suwon Hwaseong Fortress official listing)
Suwon City Tourism (suwon.go.kr/tour — Hwaseong Yeolcha trolley + admission + opening hours)
Wikipedia (Hwaseong Fortress — Jeong Yak-yong + Geojunggi crane + 5.74 km perimeter + 41 structures)

itineraryStops.name: 화성행궁
itineraryStops.description: 화성행궁은 정조 대왕이 1789~1796년 화성 축성과 함께 건립한 한국 최대 행궁(임시 이궁)입니다. 정조는 아버지 사도세자의 묘(현륭원)를 참배하기 위해 수원에 행차할 때마다 이곳에 머물렀습니다. 한국전쟁과 일제강점기에 소실됐으나 1989~2003년에 복원되었습니다. 봉수당에서 열리는 무예24기 시범 공연(화·목·토·일요일 정기 공연)을 볼 수 있습니다. 입장료 포함. 방문 시간: 약 50분.
highlights: **Korea's largest existing haenggung (detached palace)** — originally 576 rooms
King Jeongjo's 13 royal pilgrimages here to honor his executed father Crown Prince Sado
**Naknamheon = ONLY original 1796 structure remaining** (others reconstructed)
**Royal Guard Changing Ceremony Tue-Sun 11:00 AM at Sinpungnu Gate (FREE)**
1795 Royal Procession — UNESCO Memory of the World
K-drama 'Jewel in the Palace' filming location
FREE for hanbok-wearers + seniors 65+

**Naknamheon (낙남헌) is the ONLY building surviving from the original 1796 construction** — Japanese colonial-era authorities converted it into Police Station offices but did not demolish it; every other Haenggung structure visible today is a post-1996 reconstruction following the Hwaseong Seongyeok Uigwe (UNESCO Memory of the World since 2007)

**576 kan (rooms/structural bays)** = the LARGEST surviving haenggung detached-palace in Korea — King Jeongjo built it specifically to overnight when visiting his executed father Crown Prince Sado's tomb (Hyeollyungwon, 4 km south); Jeongjo made the journey 13 times between 1795-1800
timeUsed: 5 min walk from Hwaseong Fortress (Hwaseomun area)
Royal Guard Changing Ceremony at 11:00 (Tue-Sun) — ~15 min
Photo time + reassembly at vehicle (~10 min)
Optional Royal Guard Changing Ceremony at Sinpungnu Gate (Tue-Sun 11:00 AM, ~15 min, FREE — weather permitting)
itineraryStops.whyOnRoute: The temporary royal residence is INSIDE the same UNESCO site as Hwaseong Fortress, 5 min walk apart. **Naknamheon = the only authentic original structure** (others rebuilt post-1996). Time arrival for the 11:00 AM Royal Guard Changing Ceremony — FREE, 15 min, one of the catalog's strongest free-experience hooks. Haenggung occupies the **historical and emotional center** of the Hwaseong project — the fortress walls were built around it (1794-1796) precisely to defend King Jeongjo's filial-piety pilgrimages to his father's tomb. Visiting Haenggung after the wall stretch converts an abstract military-architecture lesson into lived royal-Joseon spatial experience: the same **Bongsudang throne hall** also hosted the lavish 60th-birthday banquet for Jeongjo's mother Lady Hyegyeong in 1795 (depicted in the surviving Uigwe Banchado court-painting scrolls). The site has appeared in numerous K-dramas including Jewel in the Palace (2003), Sungkyunkwan Scandal (2010), and Moonlight Drawn by Clouds (2016). A **Combined Fortress + Haenggung ticket** at 3,500 KRW (vs 2,500 KRW separately) makes Haenggung a high-value addition to the day's anchor sequence.

visitBasics.admission: Adult 1,500 / Teen 1,000 / Child 700 KRW. Combined Fortress + Haenggung 3,500 KRW. FREE for hanbok-wearers, seniors 65+
visitBasics.walking: Easy — palace grounds mostly flat with some steps between courtyards

convenience.restroom: At main entrance, near Bongsudang, at Haenggung Square
convenience.parking: Hwaseong Haenggung public parking; Haenggung Square paid lots

smartNotes.photo: **Bongsudang Main Hall** + **Naknamheon (the only original 1796 structure)** + **Royal Guard Changing Ceremony at Sinpungnu (Tue-Sun 11:00 AM)**.
smartNotes.facilities: Visitor Information Center, hanbok rental at Haenggung Square (15,000-20,000 KRW for 4 hr), Suwon Tongdak restaurants 5 min walk south.
smartNotes.tip: **Time arrival for 11:00 AM Royal Guard Changing Ceremony Tue-Sun (FREE)**. **Naknamheon is THE single most historically important spot** — the only structure not rebuilt, surviving Japanese colonial demolition. Skip Mondays — Royal Guard Changing Ceremony performed Tue-Sun only.

sources: Korea Tourism Organization (english.visitkorea.or.kr — Hwaseong Haenggung official listing)
Cultural Heritage Administration / Korea Heritage Service (cha.go.kr — Hwaseong Haenggung Historic Site No. 478)
Suwon City Tourism (suwon.go.kr/tour — Royal Guard Changing Ceremony + Mu-ye 24-gi schedule)
Visit Suwon CVB (visitsuwon.org — Suwon Pass combined ticket + Haenggung Square facilities)

itineraryStops.name: 수원 남문시장 — 점심 + 길거리 음식
itineraryStops.description: 수원 남문시장(수원남문로데오시장)은 화성 팔달문(남문) 바로 남쪽에 자리한 전통 시장으로, '남문'이란 이름도 화성 남쪽 문에서 유래했습니다. 수원의 대표 향토 음식인 수원왕갈비(1940년대부터 이어진 수원식 돼지갈비), 순대, 떡볶이 등을 즐길 수 있습니다. 화성 관람 후 자연스럽게 이어지는 위치입니다. 방문 시간: 약 50분.
highlights: **~230-year-old traditional market** — origins to King Jeongjo's Hwaseong fortress city (1796)
Korea SBA-designated 'Cultural Tourism Market'
9 sub-markets + 1,200+ shops across 10 hectares of pedestrian alleys
**Suwon Tongdak Galbi Street** — Suwon's iconic Tongdak (whole roast chicken) + Wang-Galbi (royal-sized ribs)
Yong-seong Tongdak (1972) — the most-cited Tongdak restaurant
Streetfood: sundae, tteokbokki, hotteok, bindaetteok, eomuk (3,000-7,000 KRW per dish)
**K-drama 'Reply 1988' (2015) filming alleys**
Cash recommended (20,000+ KRW)

**Suwon-style galbi (수원갈비)** is a regional Korean cuisine recognized as **distinct from Seoul-style galbi** — defined by larger 'wang-galbi (왕갈비, royal-sized)' cuts and a sweeter pear-and-ginger marinade; the **Suwon Tongdak Galbi Street (수원통닭거리)** within Nammun Market is the historical origin cluster (50+ years of continuous operation by founding families)
timeUsed: 5-min walk from Hwaseong Haenggung Square south through Paldalmun gate
Streetfood walk through Nammun Market alleys (~30-40 min)
Reassembly at vehicle near Paldalmun Plaza
Optional Suwon Tongdak Galbi lunch/dinner (₩15,000-30,000 pp; 45-60 min meal in Tongdak Galbi Street cluster)
itineraryStops.whyOnRoute: Lunch at Suwon's most famous streetfood district — leveraging proximity (5 min walk from Hwaseong Haenggung) AND the Suwon Tongdak Galbi specialty (regional cuisine you cannot get in Seoul). Picked over Haenggung Square cafes (newer, less authentic) because Nammun Market provides the Joseon-era → working-class market continuity that elevates the day's heritage narrative. **CASH WARNING** built into pre-booking communication. Located **immediately south of Paldalmun** (the only fortress gate within central Suwon), Nammun Market connects the UNESCO walled district to its commercial-living context — the market literally translates as 'south-gate market.' The 9-sub-market network (Paldalmun + Yeongdong + Jidong + Motgol + Mi-charm + Mun-Ye + Nammun Rodeo + Yeongdong Suwon + Tongdak Galbi Street) is officially designated a **Korea SBA 'Cultural Tourism Market' (문화관광형시장)** — one of ~25 markets nationwide receiving government tourism investment. Best visited around **lunch (12:00-14:00) or dinner (17:00-20:00)** to catch the Tongdak Galbi cluster + streetfood at peak operation.

visitBasics.hours: Most stalls 09:00-22:00; streetfood 12:00-late evening; Tongdak/Galbi restaurants 11:00-23:00
visitBasics.closed: Open year-round (individual stalls may close Sundays)
visitBasics.admission: Free entry; pay-as-you-go (cash recommended for streetfood; cards at sit-down restaurants)
visitBasics.walking: Easy — flat pedestrian alleys; cobbled in places

convenience.restroom: Public restrooms at Paldalmun Plaza and within larger sub-markets
convenience.parking: Suwon Nammun Market Public Parking 1,000 KRW first 30 min; coach lot at Paldalmun Plaza

smartNotes.photo: **Paldalmun (south gate of Hwaseong) framing the market entrance** — the iconic shot. **Suwon Tongdak Galbi Street** at evening illumination. **Reply 1988 filming alleys** — small 1980s-style streets at the eastern end. Hanbok-wearers browsing the market.
smartNotes.facilities: Tourist Information Center at Paldalmun Plaza, ATMs, cash exchange shops, 24-hour convenience stores. Hwaseong Fortress access via Paldalmun directly.
smartNotes.tip: **BRING CASH 20,000+ KRW** — many streetfood stalls don't accept cards. **Lunch recommendation: Suwon Tongdak (whole roast chicken) + makgeolli** — local cultural pairing, ~12,000-18,000 KRW per person at Tongdak Street. Pair with Hwaseong Fortress: Paldalmun south gate is the natural transition between fortress wall and market lunch.

sources: Korea Tourism Organization (english.visitkorea.or.kr — Suwon Paldalmun Market + Nammun Rodeo Market listings)
Visit Suwon CVB (visitsuwon.org — Nammun Market 9-sub-market network + Suwon Tongdak Galbi Street)
Korea Small & Medium Business Administration (kosbi.or.kr — Cultural Tourism Market 문화관광형시장 designation)
Wikipedia (Suwon-style galbi — wang-galbi origin + pear-and-ginger marinade)
Suwon City Tourism (suwon.go.kr/tour — Suwon Tongdak Galbi 거리 cluster + Reply 1988 filming credits)

itineraryStops.name: 스타필드 수원 별마당도서관
itineraryStops.description: 스타필드 수원 별마당도서관은 2024년 1월 26일 개관한 신세계 플래그십 스타필드 수원 내 4층 개방형 도서관입니다. 국내 스타필드 중 최대 규모(약 33만4,000㎡ 연면적)로, 중앙에 솟아오른 거대 서가 구조가 SNS 인증샷 명소입니다. 도서관 자체는 무료 입장. 쇼핑몰 푸드홀에서 점심 또는 간식을 즐길 수 있습니다. 방문 시간: 약 60분.
highlights: **Shinsegae's flagship 2nd-generation Starfield mall — opened January 26, 2024**
BCHO Partners design (also designers of original COEX Starfield Library)
**4-floor library with 22-meter bookshelves** — among Korea's tallest
~70,000 books + magazines accessible FREE
Vinyl Record Listening Lounge
Cherry blossom LED-petal installation (March-April)
Eatopia food hall, Aquafield water park, Toy Kingdom, CGV+MEGABOX cinema
Free Starfield shuttle from Suwon Station Exit 4 (~10 min)

**Rooftop installation 'The Planets' by Canadian novelist-designer Douglas Coupland** — exclusive to the Suwon location (not at Seoul COEX); positions Starfield Suwon as a **'second-generation' flagship** in Shinsegae's library-mall format, designed by **BCHO Partners (Cho Byoung-soo)** rather than the often-misattributed Dutch firm MVRDV
timeUsed: 15-min coach drive from Paldalmun Plaza to Starfield
Café break or shopping browse (~20 min)
Optional Vinyl LP cafe lounge (5F, ~₩18,500 incl. drink + headphones + LP record-player access; ~30-45 min)
itineraryStops.whyOnRoute: The 'modern Korea' counterweight after fortress + palace + market lunch. Shinsegae's flagship 2nd-generation Starfield mall opened January 26, 2024; the four-floor library with 22 m bookshelves is the architectural highlight. **All-indoor reset stop** — buffers afternoon weather risk and provides genuine multi-generational appeal before Gwangmyeong Cave's industrial-heritage finale. Starfield Library is the **single most Instagram-photographed indoor space in Greater Seoul outside central Seoul** (per Korean travel-media surveys 2024-25) — the 22m bookshelves (vs Seoul COEX's 13m) span mall floors 4F-7F as a connected atrium void. **FREE admission, no membership, photos permitted, mall-wide free entry** — a no-friction photo stop with optional cafe/dining adjacency (Eatopia food hall on B1F with 30+ Korean and international restaurants). From Suwon Station: free Starfield shuttle bus (every 10-15 min from Suwon Station Exit 4) or Subway Line 1 → Hwaseo Station Exit 1/4 (8-min walk). Mall hours **10:00-22:00 daily**, library accessible during all mall hours.

visitBasics.hours: Mall 10:00-22:00; library accessible during all mall hours
visitBasics.closed: 1-2 monthly closure days (typically Mondays — verify online)
visitBasics.admission: Library FREE; mall-wide free entry; Aquafield separate ~25,000-35,000 KRW
visitBasics.walking: Easy — escalators and elevators between all floors; wheelchair/stroller accessible

convenience.restroom: Multiple restrooms on every floor; family-friendly stations
convenience.parking: Underground ~5,000 spaces; first 3 hours FREE with mall purchase 30,000 KRW+; coach lot for groups

smartNotes.photo: **Library central atrium with 22-meter bookshelves** from 4F looking down. Vinyl Record Lounge for retro aesthetic. Cherry blossom installation in atrium (March-April). Christmas tree (December).
smartNotes.facilities: Eatopia food hall B1F, Toy Kingdom 4F, Aquafield water park B1-2F, T-Joy + CGV + MEGABOX cinemas, free WiFi, multilingual customer service, tax-refund counters.
smartNotes.tip: **Library is FREE** — exceptional value attraction. **22-meter bookshelves = Korea's tallest** (mention to guests for impact). **Cherry blossom installation March-April** is the seasonal must-see. Avoid Saturday afternoons (peak crowds). Verify Starfield monthly closure dates online.

sources: Starfield Suwon official (starfield.co.kr/suwon — opening date 2024-01-26 + library 4F-7F + free admission)
Shinsegae Property press release (Jan 2024 grand opening + 5th Starfield mall + 2nd-generation flagship concept)
Korea Tourism Organization (english.visitkorea.or.kr — Starfield Library Suwon listing)
ArchDaily — BCHO Partners (Cho Byoung-soo) Starfield Suwon design review
Wikipedia (Starfield Suwon — 22m bookshelves + Douglas Coupland 'The Planets' rooftop installation)

itineraryStops.name: 광명동굴
itineraryStops.description: 광명동굴은 1912년 일제강점기에 개발된 금·은·동·아연 광산으로, 1972년 폐광 후 2011년부터 문화 테마파크로 조성되었습니다. 연중 12°C 상시 기온으로 여름에는 냉방, 겨울에는 따뜻하게 느껴집니다. 지하 동굴 내 LED 조명 예술 작품, 와인동굴(유럽 와인 1만여 병 숙성), 황금폭포, 아쿠아월드 등이 운영됩니다. 하이라이트 구간까지 약 180계단 하강 후 동일 경로로 귀환합니다. 입장료 포함. 월요일 휴관. 방문 시간: 약 75분.
highlights: **Originally a gold/silver/copper/zinc mine** — operated 1912-1972 by Japanese colonial administration
Reopened August 2011 as Korea's first government-owned cave attraction
**Won Korea Tourism Star Award 2014** (Korea's tourism Oscar)
Total tunnel length 7.8 km; ~2 km accessible public route
**Constant ~12°C year-round** — bring light jacket
**41-meter fiberglass dragon (Yongmaru)** — Korea's tallest indoor cave sculpture
Wine Cave — Korea's largest underground wine cellar (200+ wines; tasting 5,000 KRW)
LED Art Tunnel + Fluorescent Mural + Music Hall + Cave Aquarium
Filming: 'Memories of the Alhambra' (2018), 'A Korean Odyssey'
**Closed Mondays** — Tue-Sun 09:00-18:00
Wheelchair-accessible primary route
timeUsed: 30-min coach drive from Starfield Suwon to Gwangmyeong
Themed-zone walk: Aquarium → LED Tunnel → Dragon → Music Hall → Wine Cave → Folk Park (~70-80 min)
itineraryStops.whyOnRoute: The day's industrial-heritage anchor and **all-indoor finale** — buffers any late-afternoon weather risk while delivering Korea's strongest cave-tourism experience. The 1912-1972 mining backstory + 2011 reopening + LED-art transformation is the most narratively compelling industrial-heritage story in the catalog. Wheelchair-accessible primary route makes it the most mobility-friendly heritage stop on the day. Gwangmyeong Cave's **layered industrial-heritage narrative** is the unique selling point: a 1912 Japanese-colonial-era gold/silver/copper/zinc mine operated for 60 years (1912-1972), abandoned, repurposed as shrimp-paste fermentation storage for ~40 years (thanks to the constant **~12°C / 54°F year-round cave temperature** + high humidity), and finally **purchased by Gwangmyeong City in 2011** for 4.3 billion KRW from a private owner, becoming **Korea's first government-owned cave-tourism attraction**. Won the Korea Tourism Star Award 2014. The **2 km accessible visitor route** (out of 7.8 km total tunnel length) packs Cave Aquarium + 200-m LED Art Tunnel + Wine Cave (Korea's largest underground wine cellar with 200+ Korean and imported wines) + 200-seat Music Hall + 41-meter Yongmaru fiberglass dragon (tallest indoor cave sculpture in Korea) within ~1.5-2 hours.

visitBasics.hours: Tue-Sun 09:00-18:00 (last entry 17:00); summer Jul-Aug extended weekends to 19:00
visitBasics.closed: **Mondays** (open if Mon is public holiday, closed next day instead)
visitBasics.admission: Adult 7,000 / Youth 3,500 / Child 2,000 KRW; FREE for children ≤6 and seniors 65+
visitBasics.walking: Easy to moderate — main route ~2 km, mostly level paved cave floor; wheelchair-accessible primary route

convenience.restroom: At main entrance, mid-cave (Music Hall area), and exit
convenience.parking: Large dedicated lot (~1,000 spaces); 2,000 KRW for cars / 5,000 KRW for buses; coach lot for groups

smartNotes.photo: **LED Art Tunnel** seasonal-themed lighting (spring cherry blossom, autumn foliage, winter snow) — most photographed zone. **41-m Dragon (Yongmaru)** — viral social-media spot. **Fluorescent Mural** under UV light. **Wine Cave bottle walls** with Korean wine producer labels.
smartNotes.facilities: Cave restaurant (Korean-style buffet ~15,000 KRW), café with cave-aged wine list, gift shop, Wine Cave tasting room (5,000 KRW for 3 wines), Music Hall performance schedule.
smartNotes.tip: **12°C year-round — bring light jacket** even in summer. Perfect for **summer escape from Korean heatwaves**. **Skip Mondays** — closed. **Wine tasting** (5,000 KRW for 3 wines) is the most underrated experience — Korean wines from regional wineries you can't find in Seoul. Plan 1.5-2 hours minimum; 2.5 hours with wine tasting and Music Hall concert.

sources: Korea Tourism Organization (english.visitkorea.or.kr — Gwangmyeong Cave official listing)
Gwangmyeong Cave official (gmcave.gm.go.kr — themed zones + operating hours + admission)
Gwangmyeong City Tourism (gm.go.kr/tour — 2011 city purchase 4.3 billion KRW + Korea's first govt-owned cave-tourism)
Wikipedia (Gwangmyeong Cave — 1912 Japanese colonial mine + 1972 closure + 7.8 km tunnel total)
Korea Tourism Star Award archive (Gwangmyeong Cave 2014 winner — Best New Tourist Attraction)

itineraryStops.name: 서울 귀환
itineraryStops.description: 서해안고속도로를 이용해 서울로 귀환(아침 경부고속도로와 다른 경로 — 광명의 서쪽 위치 반영). 서울 집결 지점 귀환.
highlights: Return via Seohaean Expressway from Gwangmyeong
Drop-off at Hongik University or Myeongdong
Typical arrival 18:30-19:00
timeUsed: Coach drive from Gwangmyeong to Seoul (~60 min)
itineraryStops.whyOnRoute: Return logistics — Seohaean Expressway from Gwangmyeong is faster than backtracking via Gyeongbu Expressway from Suwon, exploiting the day's geographic loop.

smartNotes.tip: Final arrival depends on Friday-evening / weekend traffic; on heavy days the drop-off may add 15-20 min.

routePhases.description: 수원화성 성벽, 화성행궁, 남문시장 — 모두 도보 거리 내.
15분 이동 후 스타필드 수원 별마당도서관 관람 및 푸드홀 점심.
30분 북서쪽 이동 후 광명동굴 LED 테마파크.

routeShapeIntro.title: How this day flows
routeShapeIntro.subtitle: A triangular Gyeonggi loop south and southwest of Seoul: Suwon's UNESCO fortress city for the morning, Suwon's flagship modern mall for early afternoon, and Gwangmyeong's transformed gold mine for the late-afternoon finale. Three different kinds of public space — military, commercial, industrial — across 250 years, in a single coach loop.

bestFor: Active travelers comfortable with full days
Travelers who want UNESCO World Heritage stops
lessIdealFor: Travelers with claustrophobia or fear of confined spaces (Gwangmyeong Cave's lower levels are narrow with low ceilings)
Anyone unable to handle ≈180 stairs down/up at Gwangmyeong's lower-level highlights (no elevator)
Travelers with limited mobility on the fortress wall — steep sections and stairs at gates
Visitors who want to see Seoul's central palaces — this trip stays entirely in Gyeonggi
Travelers who must visit on a Monday — Gwangmyeong Cave is closed for maintenance

routeLogicSections.title: Why this route is built this way

items.label: More variety than the standard Suwon tour
items.detail: Most Suwon day tours combine the fortress, palace, and Starfield Library — three stops within ten minutes of each other. This version adds Gwangmyeong Cave, a completely different landscape (underground, art-themed, year-round 12°C) that you can't easily reach from Suwon by public transit.

items.label: Indoor-friendly fallback for any weather
items.detail: Two of the four major stops — Starfield Library and Gwangmyeong Cave — are fully indoors and climate-controlled. The fortress walks are short enough to handle rain or summer heat. A reliable choice when the weather is dramatic.

items.label: Free admission, paid context
items.detail: Starfield Library is free to enter on your own — but it's much more rewarding with a guide who can explain why a mall built it (Shinsegae's MZ-generation strategy), how it differs from the COEX original, and where the best photo angles are without disturbing readers.

items.label: Cooler than a single-format day
items.detail: Heritage tours can feel relentless. Library-and-mall tours can feel light. This itinerary deliberately shifts gears — UNESCO walls, market food, modern library, then an LED-lit underground theme park — so the day doesn't fall into a single rhythm.

routeLogicSections.title: How the walking load actually feels

items.label: Fortress wall: hardest section
items.detail: ≈1 km walked with steep gradients and stairs at gates. Comfortable shoes essential. Sweeping views reward the climb but it isn't wheelchair-friendly.

items.label: Gwangmyeong Cave: ≈180 stairs each way
items.detail: Stairs to the lower-level highlights (Wine Cave, Golden Waterfall, Aqua World) and the same back. No elevator. Visitors with mobility limits stay on the upper level — still ≈40 min of content there.

items.label: Haenggung, Nammun Market, Starfield: easy
items.detail: Palace and Starfield are flat and accessible. Nammun Market has crowds at lunch but no real walking load.

items.label: Summer heat: Gwangmyeong is the relief
items.detail: 12°C constant inside the cave makes it the refuge stop on hot days. The wall walk lengthens shade breaks; market lunch shifts indoors at Starfield.

items.label: Winter cold: layer for the cave
items.detail: 12°C is cool when outside is colder. Bring a light jacket inside Gwangmyeong even in summer; in winter, expect to keep your outdoor layer on.

items.label: Mondays are not available
items.detail: Gwangmyeong Cave closes Mondays for maintenance. Tour doesn't operate. Pick the Folk Village or Waujeongsa version for Monday travel.

practicalAccordionItems.title: What's included
practicalAccordionItems.preview: Round-trip coach transport from Seoul, English-speaking tour guide, Suwon Hwaseong…
content: Round-trip coach transport from Seoul, English-speaking tour guide, Suwon Hwaseong Fortress admission, Hwaseong Haenggung Palace admission, Gwangmyeong Cave admission.
Not included: meals, drinks, optional cave-winery tasting, tips.
Starfield Library entry is free.

practicalAccordionItems.title: Pickup & drop-off
practicalAccordionItems.preview: Hongik University Station (Exit 8) at ≈08:00 or Myeongdong Station (Exit 2 or 4) at ≈08:30.
content: Hongik University Station (Exit 8) at ≈08:00 or Myeongdong Station (Exit 2 or 4) at ≈08:30.
Arrive 10 minutes early.
There is no hotel pickup; meeting points only.
Latecomers cannot be accommodated and are non-refundable.

practicalAccordionItems.title: Walking & terrain
content: Moderate.
The fortress wall walk involves uphill stone steps.
Gwangmyeong Cave has approximately 180 steps down to the lower levels and back up — there is no elevator.
Comfortable closed-toe walking shoes are essential.
Not recommended if you have significant mobility limitations.

practicalAccordionItems.title: What to wear & bring
practicalAccordionItems.preview: Comfortable walking shoes (sneakers or hiking shoes), a light jacket — Gwangmyeong Cave…
content: Comfortable walking shoes (sneakers or hiking shoes), a light jacket — Gwangmyeong Cave is 12°C year-round, so even in summer it's chilly.
In winter, bring a warm coat for the fortress wall and a lighter layer for the cave.
Camera, water, cash for lunch and street food (₩10,000–₩20,000 budget).

practicalAccordionItems.title: Cancellation
practicalAccordionItems.preview: Free cancellation up to 24 hours before the tour starts.
content: Free cancellation up to 24 hours before the tour starts.
Same-day cancellations and no-shows are non-refundable.
Tour does not operate on Mondays (Gwangmyeong Cave closed).

practicalAccordionItems.title: Children & accessibility
practicalAccordionItems.preview: Children typically enjoy this tour — the cave's lights, dragon sculpture, and Aqua World…
content: Children typically enjoy this tour — the cave's lights, dragon sculpture, and Aqua World are popular with families.
The fortress walk has stairs and uneven surfaces.
The cave's 180+ stairs make it unsuitable for guests with mobility issues, strollers, or fear of confined spaces.

practicalWeatherStatic._seasonalSummary: Suwon and Gwangmyeong track Seoul's weather closely (about 30 km south/southwest). Plan for hot, humid summers and cold, dry winters; the cave is a constant 12°C year-round, providing relief in summer and shelter in winter.

_seasonalTemperatures.notes: Cherry blossoms along the fortress wall in early–mid April.
Hot and humid; cave is a cool refuge.
Best season — clear skies, mild temperatures.
Cold winds on the fortress wall; library and cave are climate-controlled.

seasonalVariations.description: Cherry blossoms along the Hwaseong wall; cave opens spring-themed light installations.
Cave's 12°C interior is a popular escape from Seoul's humidity; Suwon Hwaseong Cultural Festival in summer.
Maple foliage along the fortress wall; major Suwon Hwaseong Cultural Festival in October.
Snow on the fortress wall is a classic photo; cave hosts year-round LED tableaux undisturbed by weather.

bookingTrustItems.title: 공인 현지 운영사
bookingTrustItems.description: Authorized Korean tour operator with full insurance

bookingTrustItems.title: 코스 전문
bookingTrustItems.description: Hand-tuned itinerary, not a generic loop
bookingTrustItems.body: 광명동굴 연중 12°C 상시 기온으로 여름 더위를 식히기에 이상적. 시즌 관계없이 즐길 수 있는 균형 잡힌 코스.

bookingTrustItems.title: 그룹 규모 관리
bookingTrustItems.body: 광명동굴 월요일 휴관을 사전 고지 — 예약 전 명확한 안내 제공.

bookingSupportSteps.title: 즉시 확인
bookingSupportSteps.description: 집결 장소·시각 포함 즉시 예약 확인서 발송 (월요일 미운행 안내 포함).

bookingSupportSteps.title: 12시간 전 알림
bookingSupportSteps.detail: Weather update and any route adjustments
bookingSupportSteps.description: 화성행궁 무예24기 공연 일정 및 광명동굴 당일 행사 사전 안내.

bookingSupportSteps.title: 최종 픽업 안내
bookingSupportSteps.detail: Exact pickup time and driver contact
bookingSupportSteps.description: 출발 전날 저녁 최종 집결 시각 확인.

bookingSupportSteps.title: 당일 코스 안내
bookingSupportSteps.detail: Morning briefing based on live conditions
bookingSupportSteps.description: 날씨 및 코스 안내 아침 메시지 전달.

bookingSupportSteps.title: 경유지별 심층 안내
bookingSupportSteps.description: 정조의 화성 건설 배경, 일제강점기 광업 역사, 별마당도서관 설계 등 가이드 심층 설명.

staticQuestions.question: 왜 월요일에는 투어가 없나요?
staticQuestions.answer: 광명동굴이 매주 월요일 정기 휴관입니다. 광명동굴이 4개 핵심 경유지 중 하나이므로, 이 투어는 화·수·목·금·토·일요일만 운행합니다.

staticQuestions.question: 입장료가 포함되어 있나요?
staticQuestions.answer: 네, 수원화성·화성행궁·광명동굴 입장료가 모두 포함됩니다. 스타필드 별마당도서관은 무료 입장입니다.

staticQuestions.question: 점심 식사가 포함되어 있나요?
staticQuestions.answer: 포함되지 않습니다. 수원 남문시장(수원왕갈비, 순대 등) 또는 스타필드 수원 푸드홀에서 즐기시면 됩니다. 예산: 약 ₩10,000~20,000.

staticQuestions.question: 광명동굴 계단이 많은가요?
staticQuestions.answer: 하이라이트 구간(와인동굴·황금폭포·아쿠아월드)까지 약 180계단 하강 후 동일 경로로 귀환합니다. 경사 완만한 구간도 있지만 전체적으로 계단이 많습니다.

staticQuestions.question: 동굴이 덥거나 춥지 않나요?
staticQuestions.answer: 연중 12°C(54°F)입니다. 여름에는 시원하게 느껴지고, 겨울에는 따뜻하게 느껴집니다. 여름 방문 시 얇은 겉옷 하나를 챙기시면 좋습니다.

staticQuestions.question: 수원+한국민속촌 투어와 어떻게 다른가요?
staticQuestions.answer: 두 투어 모두 화성·행궁·스타필드를 포함합니다. 이 투어는 광명동굴(지하 LED 테마파크)이 추가되고, 민속촌 대신 남문시장 점심이 포함됩니다.

staticQuestions.question: 수원·광명을 처음 방문하는 분에게 좋은 선택인가요?
staticQuestions.answer: 네, 유네스코 성곽(화성), 조선 이궁(행궁), 지하 테마파크(광명동굴), 현대 도서관(스타필드)을 하루에 균형 있게 볼 수 있습니다.

staticQuestions.question: 도보 이동이 힘들지 않나요?
staticQuestions.answer: 활동적인 하루이며 계단 구간이 있습니다. 화성 성벽이 가파른 구간과 계단을 포함하며(약 1km 도보), 광명동굴 하이라이트 구간은 약 180계단 왕복입니다.

staticQuestions.question: 가족 여행에 적합한가요?
staticQuestions.answer: 성벽 도보와 광명동굴 계단을 소화할 수 있는 어린이(만 10세 이상)가 동반하는 가족에게 적합합니다. 동굴 LED 전시는 어린이들이 특히 좋아합니다.

staticQuestions.question: 어르신도 참여할 수 있나요?
staticQuestions.answer: 계단과 좁은 공간이 있어, 활동적인 어르신께 권장합니다. 광명동굴 180계단은 속도를 조절해 이동합니다.

staticQuestions.question: 권장 최소 연령은 몇 세인가요?
staticQuestions.answer: 만 10세 이상 추천 — 성벽 고르지 않은 지면, 동굴 180계단, 9시간 일정을 소화할 수 있는 어린이에게 적합합니다.

walking_notes: Active walking day with some stairs and uneven terrain.

props.headlineLine1: UNESCO walls, an underground gold mine, and a four-floor library —
props.headlineLine2: Gyeonggi Province's most photographed day in 9 hours

hero.tagline: Korea's only walled fortress, a former gold mine reborn as art, and the country's most-photographed library

glanceItems.label: Walking
glanceItems.value: Moderate — fortress walk is uphill on stone, cave includes 180+ steps

glanceItems.label: Includes
glanceItems.value: Round-trip transport, English guide, Suwon Hwaseong Fortress + Haenggung Palace + Gwangmyeong Cave admissions

glanceItems.label: Lunch
glanceItems.value: Not included — typically eaten at Starfield mall food hall or Suwon Nammun Market

galleryItems.alt: Suwon Hwaseong Fortress wall section with Janganmun gate visible in the distance and bastions along the ridgeline

galleryItems.alt: Suwon Nammun Market's covered alley with food stalls selling Suwon-style galbi, fried chicken, and traditional sweets

galleryItems.location: Suwon Nammun Market, south gate of the fortress, Suwon
galleryItems.atmosphere: 220-year history; created by King Jeongjo during Hwaseong's construction; tongdak alley + 9 sub-markets

itineraryStops.name: Seoul departure
itineraryStops.description: Pickup from Hongik University Station (Exit 8) or Myeongdong Station (Exit 2 or 4). About one hour by coach south to Suwon, the old fortress city of Joseon's King Jeongjo.
highlights: Central Seoul meeting points
1-hour transfer south
timeUsed: Meet driver-guide at Seoul pickup point
itineraryStops.whyOnRoute: An early Seoul departure lets the group reach Gwangmyeong Cave before noon when underground sections are coolest and lighting effects are at their best.

_poi_meta.note: Operational pickup — Seoul departure; not a sightseeing POI

itineraryStops.name: Suwon Hwaseong Fortress
itineraryStops.description: Guided walk along a section of the 5.7 km UNESCO-listed fortress wall, built 1794–1796 under King Jeongjo as a planned new capital. Highlights typically include Janganmun (the largest gate), Hwaseomun, beacon mounds, and bastions, with sweeping city views from the wall.
highlights: UNESCO since 1997
Major fortress gates
Beacon mounds and bastions
Sweeping city views
timeUsed: Main attraction exploration with photo time
itineraryStops.whyOnRoute: The route's UNESCO anchor — Korea's only remaining walled fortress city, built 1794-1796 under King Jeongjo. The walked section between Janganmun and Hwaseomun gives the highest density of named features per minute: largest gate, beacon mound, bastions, command posts. Sweeping city views from the wall reward the climb.

visitBasics.hours: Wall accessible 24h; recommended 09:00–18:00 (summer) / 09:00–17:00 (winter); evening hours 18:00–21:30 (May–Oct, Wed–Sun)
visitBasics.admission: Fortress wall: 1,000 KRW adult / 700 KRW youth / 500 KRW child (free for children ≤6, seniors 65+, hanbok wearers, last Wed of month)
visitBasics.walking: Moderate to challenging — wall has steep sections, stairs at gates and command posts; some sections wheelchair-difficult

convenience.restroom: Available at major gates and visitor centers
convenience.parking: Available near Janganmun and Hwaseong Haenggung

smartNotes.photo: Janganmun (north gate) and Seojangdae command post offer the best wall-and-city panoramas
smartNotes.facilities: Information centers at main gates; English maps available
smartNotes.tip: Trolley (1,500 KRW adult) is good for elderly or limited-mobility visitors; runs Yeonmudae→Hwahongmun→Janganmun→Hwaseomun→Haenggung loop

itineraryStops.name: Hwaseong Haenggung Palace
itineraryStops.description: The temporary royal residence inside the fortress, used by King Jeongjo when he visited his father's tomb. The largest haenggung (detached palace) in Korea. Live performances — royal guard ceremonies, martial arts demos — run on most days.
highlights: Largest detached palace in Korea
Royal guard reenactment
Martial-arts demonstrations
timeUsed: Main attraction exploration with photo time
itineraryStops.whyOnRoute: Pairs naturally with the wall — the same UNESCO site, 5 minutes apart. Korea's largest haenggung (detached palace), restored from 19th-century fire damage. Live royal-guard ceremonies and martial-arts demos add audio/visual variety after the quiet wall walk; performance times posted at the entrance.

visitBasics.admission: Adult 1,500 KRW / Teen 1,000 KRW / Child 700 KRW (free for children ≤6, seniors 65+, hanbok wearers)
visitBasics.walking: Easy — palace grounds are mostly flat; some steps between courtyards

convenience.restroom: Available at entrance plaza and within palace grounds

smartNotes.photo: Sinpungnu main gate and Bongsudang interior with throne are most photographable; royal guard ceremony makes a great action shot
smartNotes.facilities: 3-storey tourist info center & 3D theater outside front entrance (09:00–18:00); free hanbok rental nearby
smartNotes.tip: Time your visit for the Royal Guard Ceremony (typically Sat 14:00, April–November)

itineraryStops.name: Suwon Nammun Market (lunch & street food)
itineraryStops.description: Free time at Suwon's largest traditional market — a 220-year-old market organized by King Jeongjo during Hwaseong's construction in the late 1700s, the only Korean market created by royal command. Located at Paldalmun (the fortress south gate), the market is famous for Suwon-style galbi, fried chicken (tongdak alley, ≈400 m of stalls), traditional sweets, and dozens of street-food kiosks. Nine sub-markets coexist under the Nammun umbrella; the guide recommends a few favorites if you want a sit-down lunch.
highlights: 220-year history (King Jeongjo's market, only royal-organized market in Korea)
Suwon-style galbi (signature dish from cattle-market era)
Tongdak (whole fried chicken) alley — ≈400 m of stalls
Coalition of 9 sub-markets, the largest in Gyeonggi
itineraryStops.whyOnRoute: Mid-route meal break aligned with guest energy and route timing.

_poi_meta.note: Operational stop (lunch); identified by name pattern. visitBasics/convenience/smartNotes were not factsheet-derived.

itineraryStops.name: Starfield Library, Suwon
itineraryStops.description: Inside Starfield Suwon — Shinsegae's flagship 'second-generation' Starfield mall opened January 2024 — the library spans four floors with bookshelves up to 22 meters tall, a vinyl-record listening area, and curated reading lounges. Bigger and quieter than the original Starfield Library at COEX in Seoul. Free time for photos, browsing, and coffee.
highlights: Four-floor library, 22 m shelves
Vinyl-record listening seats
Free admission (entry to library is free)
timeUsed: Main attraction exploration with photo time
itineraryStops.whyOnRoute: The 'modern Korea' counterweight to the morning's heritage stops. Shinsegae's flagship 2nd-generation Starfield mall opened January 2024; four-floor library with 22 m bookshelves and vinyl-listening seats is the day's contemporary photo. Bigger and quieter than the original COEX library. Free entry, escalator-accessible.

visitBasics.hours: Mall 10:00–22:00; library accessible during mall hours
visitBasics.closed: Open year-round (check for occasional Starfield monthly closures)
visitBasics.walking: Easy — escalators and elevators between floors

convenience.parking: Mall parking available (free for first 20 min, paid thereafter)

smartNotes.photo: View from 7F looking down at the library is the iconic shot; lower floors give the dramatic 22m bookshelf perspective
smartNotes.facilities: B2 Traders Wholesale Club, multiple cafés/restaurants, Olive Young, Innisfree, dog-friendly stores
smartNotes.tip: Located near Hwaseo Station (Line 1, exit 1); pet-friendly mall with dog-stroller rentals

itineraryStops.name: Gwangmyeong Cave
itineraryStops.description: A 30-minute drive to Gwangmyeong, where the former Siheung gold mine — opened by Japanese colonial authorities in 1912 and worked under forced labor — has been reborn as Korea's largest themed cave park. The mine produced gold, silver, copper, and zinc until closure in 1972, then served as a salted-shrimp storage facility for 40 years before Gwangmyeong city converted it into a tourist attraction in 2011. Its 7.8 km of underground tunnels now host LED-lit tableaux, an underground winery (Wine Cave, 170+ Korean wines), an Aqua World aquarium, the Golden Waterfall, the Golden Road of wishes, and Korea's largest dragon sculpture — a 41 m, 800 kg sculpture named 'Lord of the Cave' built by Weta Workshop (the New Zealand prop studio behind Lord of the Rings). Year-round 12°C inside — an air-conditioned experience in summer, a sheltered one in winter.
highlights: Former Siheung Mine (1912-1972, gold/silver/copper/zinc)
7.8 km of LED-lit underground tunnels
41m dragon by Weta Workshop (Lord of the Rings prop studio)
Underground Wine Cave (170+ Korean wines)
Year-round 12°C — relief in summer, sheltered in winter
timeUsed: Main attraction exploration with photo time
itineraryStops.whyOnRoute: The day's most distinctive contrast: a Japanese-colonial-era gold and silver mine reborn as Korea's largest themed cave park. 7.8 km of underground tunnels with LED tableaux, an underground winery, an Aqua World aquarium, the Golden Waterfall, and Korea's largest dragon sculpture (41 m). The constant 12°C inside makes it a relief stop in summer and a sheltered one in winter.

visitBasics.closed: Mondays (or Tuesday if Monday is a public holiday)
visitBasics.admission: Adult 6,000 KRW / Teen 3,500 KRW / Child 2,000 KRW (Gwangmyeong residents discounted)
visitBasics.walking: Easy to moderate — paved underground paths; some inclines

convenience.restroom: Available at entrance; limited inside the cave

smartNotes.photo: The golden dragon hall and light tunnel are the most photographed; bring a flash-capable camera as the lighting is moody
smartNotes.facilities: Wine tasting room, cave restaurant ('Cave Bistro'), gift shop
smartNotes.tip: Bring a light jacket — the cave is constantly 12°C even in summer; the cave wine selection makes for unique souvenirs

_poi_meta.note: Operational details based on aggregated tour info; verify hours seasonally

itineraryStops.name: Return to Seoul
itineraryStops.description: Coach back to central Seoul, with drop-off at the same morning meeting points. Arrival around 17:00–17:30 depending on traffic.
highlights: Drop-off in central Seoul
itineraryStops.whyOnRoute: Return logistics — drop-off chosen for guest convenience and timing certainty.

_poi_meta.note: Operational stop (return); identified by name pattern. visitBasics/convenience/smartNotes were not factsheet-derived.

routePhases.description: Seoul → Suwon city via Gyeongbu Expressway.
Suwon Hwaseong walls, Haenggung palace, and Nammun market — all within walking distance.
Drive 15 minutes to Starfield Suwon for the library and mall food hall.
Drive 30 minutes northwest to Gwangmyeong Cave for the LED-lit themed park.

routeShapeIntro.title: How this day flows
routeShapeIntro.subtitle: A triangular Gyeonggi loop south and southwest of Seoul: Suwon's UNESCO fortress city for the morning, Suwon's flagship modern mall for early afternoon, and Gwangmyeong's transformed gold mine for the late-afternoon finale. Three different kinds of public space — military, commercial, industrial — across 250 years, in a single coach loop.

bestFor: Active travelers comfortable with full days
Travelers who want UNESCO World Heritage stops
lessIdealFor: Travelers needing a tight same-day flight buffer

routeLogicSections.title: Why this route is built this way

items.label: More variety than the standard Suwon tour
items.detail: Most Suwon day tours combine the fortress, palace, and Starfield Library — three stops within ten minutes of each other. This version adds Gwangmyeong Cave, a completely different landscape (underground, art-themed, year-round 12°C) that you can't easily reach from Suwon by public transit.

items.label: Indoor-friendly fallback for any weather
items.detail: Two of the four major stops — Starfield Library and Gwangmyeong Cave — are fully indoors and climate-controlled. The fortress walks are short enough to handle rain or summer heat. A reliable choice when the weather is dramatic.

items.label: Free admission, paid context
items.detail: Starfield Library is free to enter on your own — but it's much more rewarding with a guide who can explain why a mall built it (Shinsegae's MZ-generation strategy), how it differs from the COEX original, and where the best photo angles are without disturbing readers.

items.label: Cooler than a single-format day
items.detail: Heritage tours can feel relentless. Library-and-mall tours can feel light. This itinerary deliberately shifts gears — UNESCO walls, market food, modern library, then an LED-lit underground theme park — so the day doesn't fall into a single rhythm.

practicalAccordionItems.title: What's included
practicalAccordionItems.content: Round-trip coach transport from Seoul, English-speaking tour guide, Suwon Hwaseong Fortress admission, Hwaseong Haenggung Palace admission, Gwangmyeong Cave admission. Not included: meals, drinks, optional cave-winery tasting, tips. Starfield Library entry is free.

practicalAccordionItems.title: Pickup & drop-off
practicalAccordionItems.content: Hongik University Station (Exit 8) at ≈08:00 or Myeongdong Station (Exit 2 or 4) at ≈08:30. Arrive 10 minutes early. There is no hotel pickup; meeting points only. Latecomers cannot be accommodated and are non-refundable.

practicalAccordionItems.title: Walking & terrain
practicalAccordionItems.content: Moderate. The fortress wall walk involves uphill stone steps. Gwangmyeong Cave has approximately 180 steps down to the lower levels and back up — there is no elevator. Comfortable closed-toe walking shoes are essential. Not recommended if you have significant mobility limitations.

practicalAccordionItems.title: What to wear & bring
practicalAccordionItems.content: Comfortable walking shoes (sneakers or hiking shoes), a light jacket — Gwangmyeong Cave is 12°C year-round, so even in summer it's chilly. In winter, bring a warm coat for the fortress wall and a lighter layer for the cave. Camera, water, cash for lunch and street food (₩10,000–₩20,000 budget).

practicalAccordionItems.title: Cancellation
practicalAccordionItems.content: Free cancellation up to 24 hours before the tour starts. Same-day cancellations and no-shows are non-refundable. Tour does not operate on Mondays (Gwangmyeong Cave closed).

practicalAccordionItems.title: Children & accessibility
practicalAccordionItems.content: Children typically enjoy this tour — the cave's lights, dragon sculpture, and Aqua World are popular with families. The fortress walk has stairs and uneven surfaces. The cave's 180+ stairs make it unsuitable for guests with mobility issues, strollers, or fear of confined spaces.

practicalWeatherStatic._seasonalSummary: Suwon and Gwangmyeong track Seoul's weather closely (about 30 km south/southwest). Plan for hot, humid summers and cold, dry winters; the cave is a constant 12°C year-round, providing relief in summer and shelter in winter.

_seasonalTemperatures.notes: Cherry blossoms along the fortress wall in early–mid April.
Hot and humid; cave is a cool refuge.
Best season — clear skies, mild temperatures.
Cold winds on the fortress wall; library and cave are climate-controlled.

seasonalVariations.highlight: Cherry blossoms along the Hwaseong wall; cave opens spring-themed light installations.
Cave's 12°C interior is a popular escape from Seoul's humidity; Suwon Hwaseong Cultural Festival in summer.
Maple foliage along the fortress wall; major Suwon Hwaseong Cultural Festival in October.
Snow on the fortress wall is a classic photo; cave hosts year-round LED tableaux undisturbed by weather.

bookingTrustItems.title: Licensed local operator
bookingTrustItems.description: Authorized Korean tour operator with full insurance

bookingTrustItems.title: Route specialists
bookingTrustItems.description: Hand-tuned itinerary, not a generic loop

bookingSupportSteps.title: 12-Hour Reminder
bookingSupportSteps.detail: Weather update and any route adjustments

bookingSupportSteps.title: Final Pickup Info
bookingSupportSteps.detail: Exact pickup time and driver contact

bookingSupportSteps.title: Day-of Route Notes
bookingSupportSteps.detail: Morning briefing based on live conditions

staticQuestions.question: Why isn't the tour available on Mondays?
staticQuestions.answer: Gwangmyeong Cave is closed on Mondays for maintenance. Since the cave is one of the four core stops, the tour doesn't operate on Mondays. If you want to visit on a Monday, consider the Suwon-only itineraries (Hwaseong + Folk Village or Hwaseong + Waujeongsa).

staticQuestions.question: Are admissions included?
staticQuestions.answer: Yes — Suwon Hwaseong Fortress, Hwaseong Haenggung Palace, and Gwangmyeong Cave admissions are all included. Starfield Library is free for everyone. Optional cave-winery tasting and any folk-village extras are at your own cost.

staticQuestions.question: Is lunch included?
staticQuestions.answer: No. Lunch is typically eaten during free time at Suwon Nammun Market (street food, Suwon galbi) or Starfield Suwon's food hall. Budget ₩10,000–₩20,000 per person.

staticQuestions.question: How many stairs are in Gwangmyeong Cave?
staticQuestions.answer: Approximately 180 steps down to the lower-level highlights (Wine Cave, Golden Waterfall, Aqua World) and the same back up. There is no elevator. Visitors with mobility issues or fear of confined spaces typically stay on the upper level.

staticQuestions.question: Is the cave warm or cold?
staticQuestions.answer: Cool. The cave is a constant 12°C (54°F) year-round. In summer that's a relief; in winter, you'll want to keep a layer on. Summer visitors often bring a light jacket just for this stop.

staticQuestions.question: How is this different from the Suwon + Folk Village tour?
staticQuestions.answer: Both visit the fortress, palace, and Starfield Library. This version pairs them with Gwangmyeong Cave (an underground LED-lit theme park) and Suwon Nammun Market. The Folk Village version drops the cave and adds the Korean Folk Village in Yongin (a 270-house open-air heritage park). Choose this one for variety and indoor weather; the Folk Village version for deeper Joseon-era heritage.

staticQuestions.question: Is this a good choice for first-time visitors to Suwon & Gwangmyeong?
staticQuestions.answer: Yes — strong first-time pick for travelers who want one UNESCO site (Hwaseong Fortress), one Joseon palace (Haenggung), one underground themed attraction (Gwangmyeong Cave), and one contemporary photo space (Starfield Library) in a single day. The format gives four very different cultural angles. Note: the tour doesn't operate on Mondays (Gwangmyeong Cave maintenance day).

staticQuestions.question: Is the walking difficult?
staticQuestions.answer: Active walking day with one heavy stair section. Hwaseong wall has steep sections and stairs (≈1 km walked). Gwangmyeong Cave's lower-level highlights require ≈180 stairs down and the same back up — no elevator. Haenggung Palace is mostly flat with some courtyard steps. Starfield Library is fully escalator/elevator-accessible. Total walking ≈4 km. Comfortable shoes essential, especially for the cave stairs.

staticQuestions.question: Is this tour suitable for families?
staticQuestions.answer: Yes for families whose kids can handle the wall walk and Gwangmyeong's stairs. The cave is a kid favorite — LED-lit tableaux, Aqua World aquarium, the 41 m dragon sculpture, the underground winery (parents only) and Golden Waterfall. Strollers don't work on the wall or in the cave's stair sections. Suwon Nammun Market is a hit for street-food-curious teens. Year-round 12°C inside the cave makes it a relief stop.

staticQuestions.question: Can older adults join comfortably?
staticQuestions.answer: Recommended only for active seniors comfortable with stairs and confined spaces. The 180 stairs at Gwangmyeong's lower levels have no elevator alternative — the upper level (≈40 min of content) is accessible if stairs aren't possible. The fortress wall has steep sections; easier flat segments are walkable. Haenggung and Starfield are fully accessible. Bring a light layer for the cave even in summer.

staticQuestions.question: What's the recommended minimum age?
staticQuestions.answer: Best fit is around age 10 and up. The fortress wall has uneven surfaces, the cave has 180 stairs each way, and the day is long (9 hours). Younger kids can manage if comfortable on stairs and with confined spaces (the cave's lower tunnels are narrow). Kids often love the cave's underground attractions — dragon sculpture, Aqua World, Wine Cave (visual only). The Suwon street-food market works for all ages.
